display.text:
model: gemma-4-26b
task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |
  Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model of a simple ideal pendulum using SI units.

  Coordinate system:
    +x = right
    +y = upward
    +z = out of page

  Pendulum definition:
    - Massless rod
    - Spherical bob
    - Single hinge joint

  Joint specifications:
    - Type: hinge
    - Location: [0, 1, 0]
    - Axis: [0, 0, 1]
    - Damping: 0
    - Friction: 0
    - No losses

  Rod:
    - Length: 0.2485 m
    - Mass: 0
    - Collision: disabled

  Bob:
    - Shape: sphere
    - Diameter: 0.1 m
    - Mass: 1.0 kg

  Environment:
    - Gravity: [0, -9.81, 0]

  Initial conditions:
    - Pendulum starts horizontal
    - Bob positioned along +x from hinge
    - Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical
    - Initial angular velocity: 0 rad/s

  MuJoCo requirements:
    - Enable energy computation
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Joint rotates only about z-axis
    - Use SI units

  Acceptance criteria:
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Rod length = 0.2485 m
    - Rod mass = 0 kg
    - Bob mass = 1 kg
    - Gravity = [0, -9.81, 0]
    - Initial angle = +90 degrees
    - Initial angular velocity = 0
    - Energy computation enabled

output_format:
  type: xml
  constraints:
    - Return only valid MuJoCo XML
    - No explanations
    - No additional text

state.title: YAML_creation/Temperature_Testing/V7_Temp_0.2/test28/ChatGPT-5.3-Instant_Pend.YAML

input.content:
<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>

    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <body name="rod" pos="0 -0.2485 0">
        <geom type="capsule" size="0.01" fromto="0 0 0 0 -0.2485 0" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
        <body name="bob" pos="0 -0.2485 0">
          <geom type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
        </body>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <key qpos="1.5708 0 0" name="horizontal"/>
  </keyframe>

  <equality>
    <contact/>
  </equality>

  <plugin>
    <energy/>
  </plugin>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>

    <body name="pendulum_base" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <body name="rod" pos="0 0 0">
        <!-- The rod is modeled as a geom attached to the hinge body, but since it's massless and has no collision, we can just use the bob's position relative to the hinge -->
        <!-- However, the prompt asks for a rod of length 0.2485m. In MuJoCo, a massless rod is often just the distance between the joint and the bob. -->
        <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
          <geom type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
        </body>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <!-- Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical. 
         Vertical is typically qpos=0. 
         If hinge is at (0,1,0) and bob is at (0.2485, 1, 0), qpos should be PI/2.
         Wait, the coordinate system is +y = upward. 
         If the pendulum hangs down, the bob is at (0, 1-0.2485, 0). 
         If it starts horizontal (+x), the bob is at (0.2485, 1, 0).
         In MuJoCo, qpos for a hinge joint is the angle.
         If 0 is hanging down (along -y), then +90 degrees (horizontal) would be +PI/2.
         Let's check the axis: [0, 0, 1]. 
         If qpos=0, bob is at (0, 1-0.2485, 0)? No, the body 'bob' is at pos="0.2485 0 0" relative to 'pendulum_base'.
         If qpos=0, the 'bob' body is at (0.2485, 1, 0).
         Wait, if gravity is (0, -9.81, 0), then qpos=0 (horizontal) is an unstable equilibrium.
         The prompt says: "Pendulum starts horizontal", "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical".
         If vertical is qpos=0, then horizontal is qpos=PI/2.
         Let's define the rod as a geom for completeness, even if massless.
      -->
      <key qpos="1.570796" name="horizontal"/>
    </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>

    <body name="hinge_body" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <body name="rod" pos="0 0 0">
        <geom type="capsule" size="0.001" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
        <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
          <geom type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
        </body>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <key qpos="1.570796" name="horizontal"/>
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

input.command: python3 -c "import mujoco; model = mujoco.MjModel.from_xml_path('pendulum_v3.xml'); print('Model loaded successfully')"
